content.full_text:
October 14, 1980

Ms. Harriett Linn  
16505 Cotuit  
Huntington Beach, California 92649

Dear Ms. Linn:

This letter is to confirm my advice to you last October 9, 1980 as you requested.

The question you posed was as follows: the Huntington Harbor Republican Women Federated, a registered recipient committee, wants to purchase advertisements in the local newspaper which urges voters to vote for the local republican candidates listed, must the Republican Women Federated contact each of the republican candidates and inform them of the "inkind" contribution?

I advised that since the purchase of the newspaper ads was not "at the behest" of the candidates, that is not "made under the control or at the direction of a candidate, controlled committee, etc." (2 Cal. Adm. 18215) the Huntington Harbor Republican Women Federated did not make an inkind contribution to the candidates, and, therefore, need only report the expenditure(s) for the newspaper ads on Schedule E, Payments of the Recipient Committee's campaign statement.

I hope this information is helpful. Please do not hesitate to contact me if I can be of any further assistance.

Sincerely,

Helen P. Arricola  
Political Reform Consultant
